movementModel: From a stopped position, an orthogonal command moves the orb cell by cell until the next step would leave the grid or enter a wall, or until it enters a tile whose taxonomy sets stopsMovement. The player then chooses another direction.

tileTaxonomy.meaning: Blocks entry and stops the orb on the preceding cell.
Passable grid space with no rule effect.
Neutral stopping point used to change direction.
Unique destination; evaluates objectives before victory.
Collects a level-defined fictional resource or item.
Triggers a level-defined failure or penalty.
Permits departure only in its declared direction.
Moves to the linked portal after validation.
Checks a proof, signature, quorum, or level predicate.
Consumes selected fictional inputs or commits a modeled transaction.
Represents an unavailable output; the UTXO prototype fails on contact.
Runs a level-defined fictional contract operation with a resource cost.
Adds abstract educational gas units; never a real fee or asset.
Collects or executes an ordered instruction.
Collects a fictional part of a proof representation.
Checks a level-defined source/destination message rule.
Records progress after the associated finality or validation condition passes.

levels.title: Bitcoin UTXO Vault

rules.description: Continue in the chosen direction until a wall, boundary, or stopping tile.
An available UTXO adds its fictional value once, then changes to spent.
Entering a spent output ends the attempt as an invalid double-spend path.
The transaction confirms only when selected fictional value reaches 5.
The exit opens only after the transaction objective is confirmed.

objectives.description: Collect fictional input value 5 using the two available outputs.
Activate the final transaction after reaching the target.
Reach the confirmed exit without entering the spent output.

levels.educationalConcept: A Bitcoin-style UTXO is an explicit unspent output that can be selected once as an input; the puzzle then confirms a simplified final transaction.
levels.winCondition: Collect both available fictional UTXOs, confirm the transaction, and reach the exit without touching the spent output.
failConditions: reach the exit before confirming the transaction
levels.simplificationNotes: This is not a complete Bitcoin transaction: scripts, signatures, fees, change calculation, serialization, and network confirmation are abstracted.

levels.title: Ethereum Gas Labyrinth

rules.description: Continue in the chosen direction until a wall, boundary, or stopping tile.
Every directional command consumes one abstract gas cell.
Entering a valid contract consumes its fixed abstract cost and marks it successful.
The gas cell adds two abstract units once.
Entering the invalid contract produces a revert failure for this prototype attempt.
The attempt fails if gas would fall below zero.
The exit requires both valid contracts and at least one abstract gas cell.

objectives.description: Reach the exit with at least one abstract gas cell.
Reach the exit without triggering the invalid contract.

levels.educationalConcept: Ethereum execution is metered by gas and contract calls can commit or revert; a valid route must budget resources for every operation.
levels.winCondition: Activate both required contracts and reach the exit with at least one abstract gas cell remaining.
failConditions: reach the exit before both valid contracts succeed
reach the exit with no gas reserve
levels.simplificationNotes: The counter is not a fee estimate. Opcode schedules, refunds, storage, transaction envelopes, base fee, priority fee, and real currency are not simulated.
